requiredNodeKinds.description: Callable firmware entry points, handlers, callbacks, and wrappers.
Concrete aggregate types whose state is read or written by APIs.
Alternate storage layouts that appear in packet and hardware descriptors.
Enumerated control values used by IPC, dispatch, and runtime state machines.
Alias nodes that connect code references back to their real aggregate types.
Field-level state used for parser verification of reads and writes.
Preprocessor constants that affect runtime control flow or logging cadence.
Global runtime contexts that tie dispatch, timers, and callbacks together.
Parameter nodes needed when parser output is normalized back to API signatures.
Execution threads that own signal wrappers, message handlers, and runtime wrappers.
Thread signal IDs that bridge interrupts, messages, and callback wrappers.
IRQ or QURT interrupt sources that start runtime execution.
Timer nodes that schedule delayed API execution.
Hardware or software ring endpoints that directly feed runtime handlers.
Subsystem boundaries such as WMI or offload manager.
Named hardware blocks such as TQM, WBM, REO, TCL, and CE.
Dispatch tables that route firmware commands to handler APIs.
Inter-thread message IDs used to invoke handlers across runtime threads.
Concrete log emission sites that should be queryable by API.

verificationTargets.name: intr_handler
verificationTargets.goal: Verify raw OS interrupt thread flow from IST thread into the common interrupt handler.
verificationTargets.parserGapFeedback: Missing ISTMain -> intr_handler means the backend is not capturing the first interrupt-thread edge in the runtime graph.

verificationTargets.name: wlan_thread_post_init_hdlr
verificationTargets.goal: Verify that the backend can enumerate a callback that is registered from many thread entrypoints.
verificationTargets.parserGapFeedback: Any missing registrar or wrapper edge here is a real parser gap because all registrations are present in 01880 source.

verificationTargets.name: wlan_bpf_filter_offload_handler
verificationTargets.goal: Verify callback registration, runtime dispatch, downstream call edges, field access, and logs for a real offload handler.
verificationTargets.parserGapFeedback: This target should expose callback registration, runtime dispatch, field accesses, and log extraction. Missing any of those is a parser coverage gap.

verificationTargets.name: wal_peer_execute_event_handler
verificationTargets.goal: Verify message-based handler registration across threads plus attached log extraction.
verificationTargets.parserGapFeedback: This target is the backend check for cross-thread message registration. Missing rows here mean message-delivery relationships are not being ingested.

verificationTargets.name: wmi_set_recovery_test_params_cmd
verificationTargets.goal: Verify command dispatch-table routing plus the direct call into the recovery parameter handler.

sourceAnchors.label: command-definition
sourceAnchors.contains: void wmi_set_recovery_test_params_cmd(void *pContext, A_UINT32 Command, A_UINT8 *buffer, int Length)

verificationTargets.parserGapFeedback: This target defines the minimum dispatch-table behavior the backend has to support for WMI command parsing.

verificationTargets.name: recovery_delay_timer_handle
verificationTargets.goal: Verify timer callback capture from init and timeout scheduling back to the timer handler.
verificationTargets.parserGapFeedback: Timer init/schedule/handler linkage is mandatory for delayed execution tracing. Missing it is a backend gap.

verificationTargets.name: wlan_thread_signal_route_wmac_rx
verificationTargets.goal: Verify signal and interrupt nodes around the RAC RX wrapper path.
verificationTargets.parserGapFeedback: This is the core signal-wrapper pattern for RX paths. Missing signal or interrupt nodes means the runtime graph is incomplete.

relationInventory.objective: Capture all graph relations required for DB validation of one runtime API node.
relationInventory.feedbackPolicy: If any dimension is not extracted, mark parser gap feedback and treat as backend relationship extraction defect.

verificationTargets.name: pktgen_sw2tcl_ring_signal_handler
verificationTargets.goal: Verify ring-threshold signalling from the pktgen thread into a ring handler API.
verificationTargets.parserGapFeedback: This target exists to catch missing ring-threshold and signal-wrapper relationships in the backend graph.

verificationTargets.name: wal_soc_ring_polling_timer_hdlr
verificationTargets.goal: Verify timer-driven hardware ring topology nodes for REO/WBM-facing TXDE paths.
verificationTargets.parserGapFeedback: This target forces the graph model to include ring endpoints and hardware blocks, not just APIs.

verificationTargets.name: wlan_bpf_offload_vdev_notify_handler
verificationTargets.goal: Verify that a vdev state-change notification callback registered via wlan_vdev_register_notif_handler is correctly traced to its dispatch in wlan_vdev_deliver_notif.
verificationTargets.parserGapFeedback: Missing runtime caller from wlan_vdev_deliver_notif means the vdev-notif callback pattern is not captured.

requiredPathPatterns.name: vdev-notif-primary-path
requiredPathPatterns.description: Registration and runtime invocation path for vdev notify handler

verificationTargets.name: dispatch_wlan_phyerr_cmds
verificationTargets.goal: Verify that a WMI dispatch-table handler for phyerr commands is correctly linked to WMI_DispatchCmd and the WMI_RegisterDispatchTable registration.
verificationTargets.parserGapFeedback: Missing WMI_DispatchCmd -> dispatch_wlan_phyerr_cmds edge means the WMI dispatch-table pattern is not resolved.

requiredPathPatterns.name: phyerr-wmi-dispatch-path
requiredPathPatterns.description: Registration and WMI dispatch path for phyerr command handler

verificationTargets.name: _wlan_bpf_offload_cmd_handler
verificationTargets.goal: Verify that a single WMI handler mapped to 9 CMDIDs via a dispatch table is correctly resolved to WMI_DispatchCmd and wlan_bpf_offload_register.
verificationTargets.parserGapFeedback: Missing WMI_DispatchCmd -> _wlan_bpf_offload_cmd_handler edge means the multi-CMDID WMI dispatch-table pattern is not resolved.

requiredPathPatterns.name: bpf-wmi-dispatch-path
requiredPathPatterns.description: Registration and WMI dispatch path for BPF command handler
